channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Trapdoor Android Ad Fraud Scheme Hit 659 Million Daily Bid Requests Using 455 Apps
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new ad fraud and malvertising operation dubbed Trapdoor targeting Android device users.
The activity, per HUMAN's Satori Threat Intelligence and ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft plans to improve Windows 11 driver quality in 2026
item.description: Microsoft plans to raise the quality bar of Windows 11 drivers, as drivers "sit at the heart of every Windows experience" and connect the OS to the "silicon, components, and peripherals." [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft blames macOS update for undismissible Teams location prompts
item.description: Microsoft has confirmed user reports that the Teams team collaboration app is displaying non-dismissible location prompts on some macOS systems. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: DirtyDecrypt PoC Released for Linux Kernel CVE-2026-31635 LPE Vulnerability
item.description: Proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code has now been released for a recently patched security flaw in the Linux kernel that could allow for local privilege escalation (LPE). Dubbed DirtyDecrypt (aka Dirty...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Shai-Hulud malware wave compromises 600 npm packages
item.description: Threat actors earlier today published more than 600 malicious packages to the Node Package Manager (npm) index as part of a new Shai-Hulud supply-chain campaign. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 7-Eleven confirms data breach claimed by the ShinyHunters gang
item.description: Convenience store chain giant 7-Eleven confirmed that its systems were breached in a cyberattack claimed by the ShinyHunters extortion group last month. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical Microsoft Vulnerabilities Doubled: From Exposure to Escalation
item.description: Microsoft's total vulnerability count stayed steady in 2025, but critical flaws surged year over year. BeyondTrust breaks down why attackers are increasingly focused on privilege escalation and identi...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Looking Back, Looking Forward: Digesting a Dynamic Bouillabaisse of Cyber Evolution
item.description: Dark Reading editors reflect on two decades of dramatic change — from perimeter defense to assume-breach strategies — and warn that while AI, cloud, and COVID-19 have transformed the threat landscape,...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Webinar: The hidden bottlenecks in network incident response
item.description: IT teams are increasingly overwhelmed by alerts from disconnected systems, forcing responders to manually coordinate investigations during network incidents. This webinar explores how automation and A...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: The New Phishing Click: How OAuth Consent Bypasses MFA
item.description:
In February 2026, a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform called EvilTokens went live. Within five weeks, it had compromised more than 340 Microsoft 365 organizations across five countries.
The tar...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft confirms patching issues in restricted Windows networks
item.description: Microsoft says customers in restricted network environments may encounter Windows Update failures after installing the January 2026 optional non-security preview updates. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Drupal to Release Urgent Core Security Updates on May 20, Sites Told to Prepare
item.description:
Drupal has issued an alert stating that it intends to release a "core security release" for all supported branches on May 20, 2026, from 5-9 p.m. UTC.
"The Drupal Security Team urges you to reserve t...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: SEPPMail Secure E-Mail Gateway Vulnerabilities Enable RCE and Mail Traffic Access
item.description: Critical security vulnerabilities have been disclosed in SEPPMail Secure E-Mail Gateway, an enterprise-grade email security solution, that could be exploited to achieve remote code execution and enabl...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Compromised Nx Console 18.95.0 Targeted VS Code Developers with Credential Stealer
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a compromised version of the Nx Console extension that was published to the Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) Marketplace.
The extension in question is rwl...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Popular GitHub Action Tags Redirected to Imposter Commit to Steal CI/CD Credentials
item.description: In yet another software supply chain attack, threat actors have compromised the popular GitHub Actions workflow, actions-cool/issues-helper, to run malicious code that harvests sensitive credentials a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Pushes Malicious AntV npm Packages via Compromised Maintainer Account
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a fresh software supply chain attack campaign that has compromised various npm packages associated with the @antv ecosystem as part of the ongoing Mini Shai-H...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: INTERPOL ‘Operation Ramz’ seizes 53 malware, phishing servers
item.description: More than 200 individuals were arrested for cybercrime activities during INTERPOL's Operation Ramz, which focused on the Middle East and North Africa. [...]...

item.title: Microsoft Exchange Zero-Day Under Attack, No Patch Available
item.description: CVE-2026-42897 stems from a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability and can allow an attacker to compromise Outlook Web Access (OWA) mailboxes....

item.title: SHub macOS infostealer variant spoofs Apple security updates
item.description: A new variant of the 'SHub' macOS infostealer uses AppleScript to show a fake security update message and installs a backdoor. [...]...

item.title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments

item.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
item.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovC...

item.title: Shai-Hulud Worm Clones Spread After Code Release
item.description: The release of Shai-Hulud source code spells trouble for software developers as researchers worry the self-replicating worm could scale....

item.title: 5 Steps to Managing Shadow AI Tools Without Slowing Down Employees
item.description: Many employees already use shadow AI tools at work without security review. Adaptive Security breaks down how teams can build practical AI governance without adding friction for employees. [...]...

item.title: Leaked Shai-Hulud malware fuels new npm infostealer campaign
item.description: The Shai-Hulud malware leaked last week is now used in new attacks on the Node Package Manager (npm) index, as infected packages emerged over the weekend. [...]...

item.title: INTERPOL Operation Ramz Disrupts MENA Cybercrime Networks with 201 Arrests
item.description: INTERPOL has coordinated a first-of-its-kind cybercrime crackdown across the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) that led to 201 arrests and the identification of an additional 382 suspects. The initi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Fuel Tank Breaches Expand Scope of Iran's Cyber Offensive
item.description: Security experts have long warned that insecure automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems exposed on the Internet can be tampered with by threat actors....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Exchange 0-Day, npm Worm, Fake AI Repo, Cisco Exploit and More
item.description: Monday opens with a trust problem. A mail server flaw is under active use. A network control system was targeted. Trusted packages were poisoned. A fake model page pushed a stealer. Then came the fami...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Grafana says stolen GitHub token let hackers steal codebase
item.description: Grafana Labs disclosed that hackers have downloaded its source code after breaching its GitHub environment using a stolen access token. [...]...

item.title: How to Reduce Phishing Exposure Before It Turns into Business Disruption
item.description: What happens when a phishing email looks clean enough to pass through security, but dangerous enough to expose the business after one click? That is the gap many SOCs still struggle with: the attacks ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
